seo.title: Building Code Generation with Claude Tool Use + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build code generation with Claude Tool Use + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building code generation agents with Claude's Tool Use enables powerful LLM-driven development workflows, where Claude generates, tests, and refines code iteratively. However, without proper guardrails, these agents can produce unsafe code, leak credentials, or execute malicious dependencies. Vex integration ensures reliable code generation by verifying safety, syntax, and sandbox execution at every step.

architectureOverview.heading: Code Generation Architecture with Claude Tool Use
architectureOverview.description:
The architecture centers on Claude as the core agent using tool_use for code generation, sandbox execution, and test validation. Claude receives a task, generates code via tools like `generate_code`, executes it in a sandbox via `execute_code`, validates tests with `run_tests`, and iterates based on feedback. This loop continues until tests pass or max iterations are reached.

Tool definitions include code generation (Python/JS), sandbox execution (Docker/VM isolation), and test runners. Messages are wrapped with vex.verify() to catch failures early. Parallel tool calls handle dependency resolution and multi-language support, with Vex resolving conflicts.

The agent maintains state via conversation history, with Vex persisting verified checkpoints to prevent cascading errors from early tool failures.

risks.name: Malicious code generation
risks.description: Claude generates code with syscalls, file deletion, or network requests exploiting sandbox weaknesses
risks.mitigation: Vex code_safety scan blocks dangerous patterns before sandbox execution

risks.name: Credential leakage in generated code
risks.description: Code embeds API keys, passwords, or secrets from training data or context
risks.mitigation: Vex credential scanner redacts and flags leaked secrets in generated code

risks.name: Dependency supply chain attacks
risks.description: Generated code installs malicious pip/npm packages via dynamic imports
risks.mitigation: Vex dependency_safety verifies package provenance and blocks known malicious deps

risks.name: Cascading sandbox escapes
risks.description: Early tool failures compound, allowing privilege escalation through repeated execution attempts
risks.mitigation: Vex wraps each tool_use response, preventing error propagation

implementation.title: Basic Claude Tool Use Setup
implementation.description: Initialize Anthropic client and define core code generation tools

implementation.title: Agent Loop with Tool Calls
implementation.description: Main iteration loop handling Claude's tool_use responses
implementation.code:
async def agent_loop(task: str, max_iters: int = 5):
    messages = [{"role": "user", "content": task}]
    
    for i in range(max_iters):
        response = client.messages.create(
            model="claude-3-5-sonnet-20240620",
            max_tokens=1024,
            messages=messages,
            tools=code_tools
        )
        
        if response.stop_reason == "tool_use":
            tool_calls = response.tool_use
            for tool_call in tool_calls:
                if tool_call.name == "generate_code":
                    # Handle code generation
                    pass
        
        messages.append({"role": "assistant", "content": response.content})
        
    return messages[-1]

implementation.title: TypeScript Tool Definitions
implementation.description: Define tools using Anthropic TypeScript SDK

implementation.title: Sandbox Execution Handler
implementation.description: Execute generated code with timeout and resource limits
implementation.code:
import subprocess
import tempfile
import os

def sandbox_execute(code: str, timeout: int = 30) -> dict:
    with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix='.py', delete=False) as f:
        f.write(code.encode())
        f_path = f.name
    
    try:
        result = subprocess.run(
            ['docker', 'run', '--rm', '--memory=256m', '--cpus=1',
             '-v', f'{f_path}:/code.py', 'python:3.11-slim',
             'python', '/code.py'],
            capture_output=True,
            timeout=timeout,
            text=True
        )
        return {'success': True, 'stdout': result.stdout, 'stderr': result.stderr}
    finally:
        os.unlink(f_path)

vexIntegration.title: Wrap Tool Use with Vex Verify
vexIntegration.description: Protect messages.create() responses with comprehensive verification
vexIntegration.code:
import vex

@ vex.verify([
    vex.code_safety(),
    vex.syntax_validity(),
    vex.no_credential_leakage(),
    vex.execution_sandboxing(),
    vex.dependency_safety()
])
def safe_claude_call(task: str):
    response = client.messages.create(
        model="claude-3-5-sonnet-20240620",
        max_tokens=1024,
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": task}],
        tools=code_tools
    )
    
    # Extract and verify generated code from tool calls
    for tool_use in response.tool_use:
        if tool_use.name == "generate_code":
            code = tool_use.input["code"]
            verified_code = vex.verify(code, checks=[
                "code_safety",
                "syntax_validity",
                "no_credential_leakage"
            ])
            return verified_code
    
    return response
